Text:
![](_page_0_Picture_2.jpeg)

## **SHIP CONFIRM**

Once the shipment has been physically shipped, the Delivery must be 'Ship Confirmed' to close it. This will also trigger the invoicing process.

**Step 1:** Using Oracle Forms, navigate to the 'Shipping Transactions' Form

**Step 2:** Select the 'Deliveries' radio button

Enter the Delivery number in the 'From Delivery Name' and 'To Delivery Name' fields

![](_page_0_Figure_8.jpeg)

**Step 3:** Make sure you are on the 'Deliveries' Tab Click on the 'Ship Confirm' button.

**Step 4:** Accept all the default settings and click on 'OK'

![](_page_0_Figure_11.jpeg)

The following message will be displayed:

![](_page_0_Figure_13.jpeg)

Click on 'OK' again.

Document: WI - Outbound - Ship Confirm v2.0.docx

![](_page_0_Figure_15.jpeg)

![](_page_0_Figure_16.jpeg)

The status for the Delivery will be updated to 'Closed.'

Page 1 of 2 April 8, 2025

![](_page_1_Picture_2.jpeg)

## **TRIP CONFIRM**

Trip confirming is confirming multiple LPNs simultaneously instead of delivery at a time.

Note: Before confirming a Trip,

**Step 1:** Ensure every delivery on the trip has the same shipping method in the "Contents by Delivery" tab.

![](_page_1_Figure_7.jpeg)

**Step 2:** Ensure the LPNs show as "Loaded to Dock" in the "Contents by Line/LPN" tab.

![](_page_1_Figure_9.jpeg)

**Step 3:** While on the 'Trip' page, select Ship Confirm.

![](_page_1_Figure_11.jpeg)

**Step 4:** Uncheck the 'Create Master Bill of Lading' box and click 'OK'.

![](_page_1_Figure_13.jpeg)

Document: WI - Outbound - Ship Confirm v2.0.docx
